text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. Imagine reorganizing a messy closet: you don't throw away your clothes, you just arrange them better. This makes the code cleaner, easier to read and more maintainable. Over time, quick fixes and workarounds create "technical debt" – messy code that slows down development. Refactoring pays off that debt by simplifying complex parts, removing duplication, and improving naming. While it doesn't add new features, regular refactoring keeps a codebase healthy, making future changes faster and reducing bugs. It's like cleaning as you cook, saving much more time than a huge cleanup later.
